rulings.Case Title: WILL FREDERICKS ET AL VS. ROBERT FELTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Cross-Complainant Robert Felter'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To The First Affirmative Defense Of The Amended Answer Of Cross-Defendants Compass California Ii, Inc., Pete Brannigan, And Silvia Zeng
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 24, 2024 line 1. CROSS-COMPLAINANT ROBERT FELTER'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AS TO THE FIRST AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE OF THE AMENDED ANSWER OF CROSS-DEFENDANTS COMPASS CALIFORNIA II, INC., PETE BRANNIGAN, AND SILVIA ZENG is GRANTED as to the purported first affirmative defense as it alleges no new facts constituting an affirmative defense (Protect Our Benefits v. City and County of San Francisco (2015) 235 Cal.App.4th 619, 638) The Court notes that this motion does not advance this matter in any meaningful way as the sufficiency of the allegations in the operative Cross-Complaint cannot be addressed by challenging the sufficiency of the allegations regarding an affirmative defense stating to the contrary. The sufficiency of the allegations in the operative Cross-Complaint are not before this Court on this motion re: Answer to Cross-Complaint. It remains the Cross-Complainant's burden to plead and prove their case whether the purported first affirmative defense remains in the answer or not. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DOLORES PADILLA REYES ET AL VS. CARLOS MARIO ZAPATA
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 24, 2024 line 6. DEFENDANT CARLOS ZAPATA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Defendant filed a general demurrer arguing that the entire complaint fails for failure to state a proper cause of action for unlawful detainer based on non-payment of rent re: CCP 1161(2) compliance. Unlawful detainer based on non-payment of rent is only one of four causes of action asserted in this complaint. Moving party failed to carry their burden as show that complaint fails to state any cause of action. According to the Notice of Demurrer Defendant did not only challenge the second cause of action for non-payment of rent, but the entire complaint. Points and Authorities do not support a demurrer to the entire complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
